input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B9-polymer-adiabatic-force-law
Title: B9. Polymer Adiabatic Force Law

Problem:
Freely jointed polymer chain with n mass points joined by struts of length l.
What is the force law F(x) when thermally isolated?

Grading target:
Exact formula match on coefficient and powers.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Reformulate the question into a structured technical specification that guides the next agent through a logical derivation process. Break the problem down into its fundamental physical principles and the necessary mathematical steps required to reach the exact solution. Ensure the objective and all constraints remain intact, but present them as a sequence of analytical requirements.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}
variables.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

orchestration.decision_notes: The user request requires transforming a physics problem into a formal technical specification for a derivation agent. The problem involves statistical mechanics and thermodynamics of a polymer chain under adiabatic constraints.

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B9-polymer-adiabatic-force-law
Title: B9. Polymer Adiabatic Force Law

Problem:
Freely jointed polymer chain with n mass points joined by struts of length l.
What is the force law F(x) when thermally isolated?

Grading target:
Exact formula match on coefficient and powers.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

results.content:
The user request requires transforming a physics problem into a formal technical specification for a derivation agent. The problem involves statistical mechanics and thermodynamics of a polymer chain under adiabatic constraints.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Technical Specification: Derivation of the Adiabatic Force Law for a Freely Jointed Chain. Objective: Derive the exact analytical expression for the force F(x) exerted by a freely jointed polymer chain of n mass points (connected by n struts of length l) as a function of end-to-end distance x, under the constraint of thermal isolation (adiabatic process). Physical System Definition: 1. Model: A freely jointed chain (FJC) consisting of n rigid segments of length l. 2. Degrees of Freedom: The configuration is defined by the orientation of each segment. 3. Thermodynamic Constraint: The system is thermally isolated. The derivation must account for the change in internal energy/entropy such that the process follows an adiabatic path (dQ = 0), rather than an isothermal path. Analytical Requirements & Derivation Steps: 1. Partition Function Derivation (Isothermal Baseline): Start with the canonical partition function Z(x, T) for a freely jointed chain at constant temperature T. Utilize the central limit theorem approximation for large n to express the probability distribution of the end-to-end distance x. Relate Z(x, T) to the Helmholtz free energy A(x, T) = -kB T ln Z(x, T). 2. Equation of State (Isothermal Force): Derive the isothermal force F_iso(x, T) = (dA/dx)_T. For the FJC in the linear regime (small x/nl), identify the relationship F_iso proportional to x. 3. Adiabatic Constraint Application: Define the adiabatic condition using the First Law of Thermodynamics: dU = dQ - dW. For an adiabatic process, dU = -F dx. Use the relation between internal energy U, entropy S, and temperature T: dU = T dS - P dV (or in this context, dU = T dS - F dx). Apply the adiabatic condition dS = (1/T) dU or use the relation for the adiabatic exponent/index if applicable to the specific degrees of freedom. Crucial Step: Determine how the temperature T evolves as a function of x during the adiabatic compression/extension to maintain dS = 0. 4. Force Law Synthesis: Combine the temperature dependence T(x) with the isothermal force expression to find the total force F_adia(x) = (dU/dx)_S. Alternatively, use the relation F_adia(x) = F_iso(x, T) + T (dS/dx)_T and solve for the adiabatic path. Target Output Format: FINAL_ANSWER: An exact formula for F(x).
